# IBCCF — International Blockchain Complaints Forum

> IBCCF is a public fraud-prevention and blockchain-platform verification portal.
> It publishes complaint intake and case-management guidance, platform
> verification tools, a moderated community forum, and legal/regulatory
> resources for victims of blockchain and crypto-related fraud.

Canonical domain: https://ibccf.site/

This file follows the llms.txt convention (https://llmstxt.org/) to help
AI assistants and retrieval-augmented systems prioritize the site's public,
citable content. It complements, and does not replace, /robots.txt and
/sitemap.xml.

## Primary Content

- [Home](https://ibccf.site/): Overview of IBCCF's mission, services, and how
  to report or verify blockchain-related fraud.
- [Platform Verification](https://ibccf.site/verify): Look up and verify
  whether a blockchain/crypto platform is recognized, flagged, or under
  review.
- [Community Forum](https://ibccf.site/community): Public discussion threads
  where fraud victims and community members share experiences and guidance.
- [Request Access](https://ibccf.site/request-access): How to request a case
  access key to begin a complaint or recovery case.
- [Legal Resources](https://ibccf.site/legal-resources): Regulatory and legal
  guidance related to blockchain fraud complaints.
- [Withdrawal Guide](https://ibccf.site/withdrawal-guide): Step-by-step
  guidance on the fund withdrawal process for verified cases.
- [Divisions](https://ibccf.site/divisions/aml): Overview pages for IBCCF's
  operating divisions, including AML, cyber, recovery, compliance,
  intelligence, and support (see /divisions/<id> for each).
- [Contact Admin](https://ibccf.site/contact-admin): How to reach IBCCF
  administrators with questions not covered elsewhere.

## Policies

- [Privacy Policy](https://ibccf.site/privacy-policy)
- [Terms of Use](https://ibccf.site/terms-of-use)

## Machine-Readable Discovery

- [Sitemap](https://ibccf.site/sitemap.xml)
- [Robots](https://ibccf.site/robots.txt)

## Exclude

The following areas require authentication, contain private case data, or
are operational/administrative surfaces. They are not intended for AI
crawling, citation, or summarization:

- /portal — authenticated case-holder portal (private case data)
- /dashboard — authenticated secure portal entry point
- /admin — administrative dashboard and all sub-paths (/admin/mirror,
  /admin/mobile, /admin/support)
- /api/ — application programming interfaces, not human- or AI-readable content
